title: Tricalysia abnormis
intro: Tricalysia abnormis is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the Rubiaceae or the madder family, which is a genus of flowering plants. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Africa, to treat various health issues such as fever, malaria, and respiratory problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tricalysia abnormis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Tricalysia abnormis is often prepared as a leaf decoction, a root extract, or a powder, which is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine and has been studied for its potential as a natural remedy.
taxonomy: Tricalysia abnormis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family includes over 13,000 species of plants, many of which are economically important or have medicinal properties. The family is found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world, with the majority of species found in Africa and Asia. Tricalysia abnormis is native to Africa.
